meaning_in_report: Age, sex and grade are treated as developmental and demographic markers.
reporting_rule: Final public models can report this level directly when stable.

meaning_in_report: Knowledge, attitudes, perceived risk, subjective norms and self-efficacy are interpreted as psychosocial mechanisms.
reporting_rule: Reported as exploratory unless model stability is sufficient.

meaning_in_report: Family support, monitoring and household context are interpreted as interpersonal protective or vulnerability factors.
reporting_rule: Reported as exploratory when richer models are unstable.

meaning_in_report: Peers and relationships are interpreted as sources of social pressure, norms and support.
reporting_rule: Reported as exploratory when richer models are unstable.

meaning_in_report: School connection and climate are interpreted as contextual protective or vulnerability factors.
reporting_rule: Reported as exploratory when richer models are unstable.

meaning_in_report: Sexual initiation, contraception/protection, pregnancy and STI-related indicators are interpreted as outcomes.
reporting_rule: Reported by outcome domain, with rare outcomes treated cautiously.
